supply_chain.dependencies: Cencora supplies and distributes substantially all generic and branded pharmaceutical products to the U.S. Retail Pharmacy segment pharmacies. Single wholesaler dependency for pharmaceutical products.
supply_chain.risks: Disruption in global supply chain could negatively impact ability to provide products and services. Loss or disruption of supply arrangements due to health epidemics, pandemics, labor disputes, loss of key manufacturing sites, inability to procure raw materials, quality control issues, ethical sourcing issues, supplier financial distress, natural disasters, looting, vandalism, acts of war, foreign conflict, or terrorism. Dependence on single wholesaler (Cencora) for substantially all branded and generic pharmaceutical products creates operational and financial risk if Cencora experiences disruptions.

regulatory_risks: Changes in healthcare industry and regulatory environments may adversely affect business
Regulations relating to Medicare, Medicaid and other publicly financed health benefit plans
Regulations prohibiting kickbacks, beneficiary inducement and submission of false claims
Stark Law compliance
HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act) compliance
ACA (Affordable Care Act) compliance and potential changes
IRA (Inflation Reduction Act of 2022) impact on drug pricing and Medicare rebates
FDA (U.S. Food and Drug Administration) regulations
DEA (U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration) regulations on controlled substances
FTC (U.S. Federal Trade Commission) regulations
U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission regulations
Licensing and registration requirements for pharmacy operations
Federal 340B drug pricing program regulations
State and local pharmacy regulations
Foreign regulatory requirements in countries of operation
Data privacy and security laws including GDPR in UK and Germany
Environmental, health and safety regulations
Employment and labor law compliance
Anti-kickback laws and false claims laws
Laws against corporate practice of medicine
Reimbursement policy changes by government and private payors
Changes in Medicare Part D and Medicaid reimbursement rates
Potential changes to pharmaceutical pricing benchmarks (e.g., NADAC)
Medical malpractice and professional liability regulations
Opioid-related regulatory investigations and litigation
DOJ (Department of Justice) investigations
State Attorneys General investigations
Potential suspension or exclusion from government programs
Loss of required government certifications or licenses
OECD Pillar Two Global Anti-Base Erosion Rules (15% global minimum tax)
Tax law changes in U.S. and foreign jurisdictions
Trade regulations and tariffs
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) compliance
UK Bribery Act compliance
Export control and economic sanctions laws
Anti-money laundering laws

sanction_risks: U.S. and China trade relations and tariffs
Impact of war in Ukraine on operations
Impact of conflict in the Middle East
Red Sea crisis affecting delivery routes
